---
title: Environment
---

`q.env` keeps environment setup explicit. Importing qrt never reads a `.env`
file or changes the process environment.

## Load a `.env` file

```python
import qrt as q

q.env.load()                 # search upward for .env
q.env.load("config/dev.env")
q.env.load(override=True)    # replace variables already in os.environ
```

Existing process variables win by default, matching `python-dotenv` behavior.
This is useful for keeping deployment configuration authoritative while still
loading local API keys during research.

## Read variables

Use `get` for optional configuration and `require` when execution cannot
continue without a value:

```python
cache_host = q.env.get("CACHE_HOST", "localhost")
api_key = q.env.require("MARKET_DATA_API_KEY")
```

`require` raises `KeyError` with the missing variable name. To inspect a file
without changing `os.environ`, use `q.env.values(".env")`.

Do not commit `.env` files containing credentials. Commit a `.env.example`
with variable names and safe placeholder values instead.
